text: Document-level sentiment classification is a fine-grained task, and identifying intrinsic relationships between sentences to produce a suitable global representation is crucial. This work introduces a new neural network model for the purpose of generating vector representation of documents using a unified, bottom-up approach. It learns the features of individual sentences in two different ways, namely using convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and long short-term memory (LSTM) networks. The features, along with the relationships between sentences, are fused using a gated recurrent network (GRN) to generate a document vector representation. We experiment on four datasets from IMDB and Yelp Dataset Challenge. Our experimental results are better than all other baselines. Moreover, it is proved that GRN outperforms RNN when modelling the document structure for the purpose of sentiment analysis.
